name: Prone Reverse Fly
resistance_profile: Tension peaks with the arms out at shoulder height, where gravity's moment arm is longest; the hanging (stretched) bottom position is almost unloaded.
cues: Face down on a bench or floor
Raise the arms out and back, thumbs up
execution_steps: Lie face down on a flat bench (or the floor) with your arms hanging toward the ground.
Optionally hold light dumbbells and keep a slight, fixed bend in your elbows.
Raise your arms out to your sides in a wide arc, thumbs up, until they reach shoulder height.
Squeeze your rear delts at the top, then lower slowly back to the start.
good_when: You are training at home with little or no equipment.
You want a beginner-friendly way to groove the rear-delt fly pattern.
You want chest support that removes momentum and lower-back involvement.
bad_when: You need meaningful load — bodyweight or light dumbbells cap resistance quickly.
You want tension in the stretched position — gravity unloads the bottom of the range entirely.
